summary: Substantially decompiled `src/melee/gm/gmtou.c`, the guessed Tournament game-mode file. The PR replaced several placeholders with C for Tournament UI/setup, per-frame input, bracket/result handling, audio preload, and scene-entry logic, while correcting `TmAnimTimers` and `TmBoxArrays` declarations in the static header. The automated decomp.dev report recorded +2608 matched code bytes and +64 matched data bytes, with 100% matches for `fn_8019BA08`, `fn_8019C744`, `fn_8019DD60`, and `.bss`, plus high-percentage improvements for `fn_8019D1BC`, `gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame`, `gm_8019E634`, `fn_8019C048`, and `gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter`.

key_files.role: Main Tournament game-mode decompilation work; added includes, externs, and C bodies for multiple previously-placeholder functions.
evidence: New or advanced functions include `fn_8019BA08`, `fn_8019C048`, `fn_8019C744`, `fn_8019D1BC`, `fn_8019DD60`, `gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame`, `gm_8019E634`, and `gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter`.

key_files.role: Corrected static Tournament timer layout used by the new code.
evidence: `TmAnimTimers` changed from a rough layout with `x20[4]` to a more precise byte/array layout with `x10[4]`, `x18[4]`, `x1D[4]`, and `x38[4]`.

what_changed: Added missing dependencies for the decompiled code, including baselib fog/GObj object/GX link headers, DObj/MObj headers, Dolphin OS, and several gm headers such as `gm_1601.h`, `gm_1A3F.h`, `gm_1A45.h`, and `gmmain_lib.h`.

Corrected `lbl_804771B8` from `TmBoxArrays*` to a `TmBoxArrays` object and updated `lbArchive_80016DBC("TmBox.dat", ...)` to pass addresses like `&lbl_804771B8.box2`, `&lbl_804771B8.box3`, and `&lbl_804771B8.box4`.
Added local declarations for previously-needed extern data/functions: `lbl_804D663C`, `struct lbl_803DA2E0_t`, `lbl_803DA2E0`, `gm_80477738`, `lbl_803B7D3C`, `lbl_804DA948`, and `fn_80166CBC`.

Implemented `fn_8019BA08`, which traverses child/next `HSD_JObj`s and uses Z translation values such as `10000.0f` and `0.0f` to hide/show Tournament box children based on `tmd->x4B8[idx]` and `lbl_803DA2E0.x0`.

Implemented or advanced per-player UI/animation code in `fn_8019C048` and `fn_8019C744`, including controller-error hiding, text scaling through `HSD_SisLib_803A7548`, frame windows from `lbl_803B7D18`, state transitions in `lbl_80479A58.x1D`, and Y translation playback from `lbl_803DA2E0.x20`.
Updated existing code paths that referenced `lbl_80479A58.x20[idx].x0` to use the corrected `lbl_80479A58.x1D[idx].x0` field.

Implemented the large setup routine `fn_8019D1BC`, creating Tournament model GObjs with `fn_8019035C`, positioning them for 2/3/4 player counts, removing selected material animations with `HSD_MObjRemoveAnimByFlags`, creating player-name text with SisLib, and caching animation-derived Y translations into `lbl_803DA2E0.x20`.

Implemented `fn_8019DD60`, which initializes the Tournament scene view: optional announcer audio, camera/lights, models, SisLib text object setup, fog GObj creation with `HSD_FogLoadDesc`, and reset of `lbl_80479A58` timer/state fields.

Implemented `gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame`, covering Tournament select per-frame logic: menu escape handling, all-player confirmation timing, abort-combo handling, character cycling, color changes, ready/cancel transitions, stage selection, and `TmVsData` construction before `fn_8019EE80`.

Implemented `gm_8019E634` and `gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter` enough to improve matching: result sorting with parallel index arrays, handicap adjustment through `gmMainLib_8015CC34()` and `fn_80169000`, bracket-position assignment, copying `x37` data into `x4B8`, OS debug output, character/stage audio preloading, archive loads for `GmTou1p`/`GmTou4p`, and `SIS_TournamentData` setup.
smart_moves: The `TmAnimTimers` layout correction was small but high-leverage: it aligned later field accesses around `x1D`, fixed existing references, and helped the `.bss` reach 100% per the automated report.

Changing `lbl_804771B8` from pointer-to-struct to struct object fixed the archive varargs call shape without broad churn, and the diff shows the precise address-taking needed for `tournament_box2_array`, `tournament_box3_array`, and `tournament_box4_array`.

The code keeps uncertain names as `fn_...`, `lbl_...`, and offset fields rather than inventing semantic names; this is appropriate because the PR body has no explanatory notes and the file comment itself says the filename/Tournament identification is guessed from strings.

The implementations preserve matching-oriented structure: explicit local table copies, repeated search loops with `goto found` labels, exact byte casts, and `PAD_STACK` calls instead of refactoring into cleaner but different code.

The UI setup samples a model animation across frames and stores `HSD_JObjGetTranslationY` results into `lbl_803DA2E0.x20`; later `fn_8019C744` reuses that table for per-player Y translation, avoiding duplicated animation logic while matching observed data flow.
The PR limits header changes to the local static header and minimal extern declarations in `gmtou.c`, making progress on a large game-mode file without forcing premature global type exposure.
decomp_lessons: For archive-load globals, verify whether a symbol is the object or a pointer to the object. Here `TmBoxArrays lbl_804771B8` plus `&lbl_804771B8.boxN` matched better than `TmBoxArrays*` plus `->boxN`.

Offset-accurate timer structs matter even before semantic naming is known. Replacing a vague `x20[4]` layout with `x1D[4]`, padding, and separate byte arrays made existing and new code agree on offsets.
Menu/game-mode matching often needs signed controller-error checks exactly like `(s8)(u8) HSD_PadMasterStatus[idx].err != 0`, not a simplified boolean test.
Local copies of data-label tables can be necessary for codegen. `fn_8019C048` copies `lbl_803B7D18[9]` into a stack `table[9]` and then indexes it through a `u16*` cast.

Large selection-screen handlers may require preserving repetitive original control flow. The `gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame` code uses repeated `gm_8018F634()` calls, repeated loops over `x37`, and `goto` labels to match instead of abstracting lookup helpers.
Exact float constants are part of the match. This PR keeps values such as `666.0f`, `5.999997f`, `-2.799999f`, `0.054945f`, and placement formulas for 2/3/4 players.
Stack padding remains a practical tactic for near-complete functions in this file: `PAD_STACK(0xA0)`, `PAD_STACK(16)`, and `PAD_STACK(4)` appear in the new implementations.

When decompiling UI model setup, exact JObj traversal order and material-animation calls can matter; this PR repeatedly uses `HSD_JObjGetChild`, `HSD_JObjGetNext`, and `HSD_MObjRemoveAnimByFlags(c->u.dobj->mobj, 4)` rather than hiding traversal behind helpers.
naming_conventions: Lifecycle functions keep the project convention suffixes `OnEnter`, `OnFrame`, and `OnLeave`, e.g. `gm_8019B8C4_OnEnter`, `gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame`, `gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter`, and `gm_8019EE54_OnLeave`.
Unidentified functions and data remain address-based (`fn_8019BA08`, `fn_8019D1BC`, `lbl_80479A58`, `lbl_803DA2E0`) until semantics are proven.
Temporary layout structs may be named after their data label when only the layout is known, as with `struct lbl_803DA2E0_t`.
Struct fields retain offset-style names (`x0`, `x4B8`, `x37`, `x524`, `x1D`) where the PR does not establish semantic names.

Asset and archive strings preserve original casing and names: `GmTou1p`, `GmTou3p`, `GmTou4p`, `TmBox.dat`, `ScGamTour_scene_data`, `SIS_TournamentData`, `tournament_box2_array`, `tournament_box3_array`, and `tournament_box4_array`.
`TmAnimTimers.x1D` received the comment `per-player jobj/anim states`, but most subfields inside it remain byte/offset names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` in new large functions to control stack frame shape: `PAD_STACK(0xA0)` in `fn_8019D1BC`, `PAD_STACK(16)` in `fn_8019DD60`, and `PAD_STACK(4)` in frame/enter code.
Copied static data labels into local stack arrays before use: `lbl_803B7D18[9]` into `table[9]` and `lbl_803B7D3C[4]` into `indices[4]`.
Used explicit casts to match integer widths and signedness, including `(s32)`, `(u8)`, `(s8)`, `(u16*) table`, and `CSSIconHud` casts.
Preserved repetitive lookup loops with `goto found` labels in `gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame` and `gm_8019E634`, likely to match original branch layout.
Kept exact float literals and formulas for player placement and sentinel positions, including `13.0f`, `6.5f`, `-19.5f`, `73.0f`, `240.0f`, `480.0f`, `666.0f`, and `0.001f`.
Used exact HSD object access patterns (`GET_JOBJ(gobj)` in some functions and `gobj->hsd_obj` in `fn_8019C048`) rather than normalizing all call sites.
Matched UI visibility through JObj flags and translation tricks: `JOBJ_HIDDEN`, `HSD_JObjSetTranslateZ(..., 10000.0f)`, and `HSD_JObjSetTranslateZ(..., 0.0f)`.

review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report: matched code 64.43% (+0.07%, +2608 bytes) and matched data 38.39% (+0.01%, +64 bytes). New 100% matches were `fn_8019BA08` (+1296), `fn_8019C744` (+756), `fn_8019DD60` (+556), and `.bss` (+32).

Reported improvements included `fn_8019D1BC` to 98.71%, `gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame` to 97.14%, `gm_8019E634` to 90.94%, `fn_8019C048` to 84.12%, `gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter` to 98.98%, plus `.sdata2`, `.data`, `.sdata`, and `gm_8019B8C4_OnEnter`.
No PR body, human reviews, or line review comments were present in the provided slice, so no human style or naming feedback could be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `fn_8019D1BC` to see how the remaining 1.29% mismatch was resolved.
Search later PRs for `gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame` and `gm_8019E634` for cleanup or final matching of Tournament select flow.
Find refinements or semantic renames for `TmAnimTimers.x1D`, `x10`, `x18`, and `x38`.
Find where `lbl_803DA2E0` is later typed or renamed; this PR only gives a local layout with `x0[0x20]` and `x20[0x29]`.
Search for other `lbArchive_80016DBC` varargs fixes involving struct objects versus pointer-to-struct declarations.
Compare other gm/menu PRs using `PAD_STACK`, local table copies, and repeated `goto found` loops for matching input state machines.
Look for later naming of `TmData.x4B8`, `TmData.x37`, `TmData.x524`, and `TmData.x534` to improve Tournament data readability.
